title: Shortcut Edits
intro: Capital-letter shortcuts pack an operator and a motion into one key: `D` deletes to the end of the line, `C` does the same and enters Insert mode, `s` replaces the character under the cursor, and `S` rewrites the whole line.

exercises.instruction: Delete the comment: with the cursor on the `//`, press `D`.
exercises.initialCode: const debug = true; // remove this note
hints: `D` is shorthand for `d$` — it deletes from the cursor to the end of the line.

exercises.instruction: Rewrite the value: `C` on `oldValue`, then type `newValue;` and press `Esc`.
hints: `C` deletes to the end of the line and drops you straight into Insert mode.

exercises.instruction: Turn `cat` into `bat`: with the cursor on the `c`, press `s`, type `b`, then `Esc`.
hints: `s` deletes the character under the cursor and enters Insert mode.

exercises.instruction: Rewrite the whole line: press `S`, type `done();`, then `Esc`.
hints: `S` clears the entire line and enters Insert mode, no matter where the cursor is.

exercises.instruction: Rewrite from the cursor: `C` on `oldResult`, type `newResult;`, then `Esc`.
hints: `C` = delete to end of line + Insert mode.

exercises.instruction: Fix the typo `trie` → `true`: put `s` to work on the wrong character.
hints: The cursor starts on the bad character — `s`, type the right one, `Esc`.
